name: Detection of Malicious IOC in Flows
notes:
This rule triggers when an IOC is categorized as malicious in a reference set collection.

Note: The rules Malware as a Service Hash IOC in Flows, Ransomware: Ryuk IOC in Flows have been excluded from this rule to avoid repetition, their purpose is to have a dedicated rule response.
conditions_text: when a flow matches any of the following Malware as a Service Hash IOC in Flows, Ransomware: Ryuk IOC in Flows
when the flow matches File Hash is not N/A
when the flow matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes MD5', "File Hash") OR REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes SHA', "File Hash") AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Detection of Malicious IOC in Flows on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when a flow matches any of the following Malware as a Service Hash IOC in Flows, Ransomware: Ryuk IOC in Flows
AND when the flow matches File Hash is not N/A
AND when the flow matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes MD5', "File Hash") OR REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes SHA', "File Hash") AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Detection of Malicious IOC
Event description: Detects when an IOC is categorized as malicious in a reference set collection.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
